message:
test(cleanup): adversarial shapes for cleanup_target_path_is_safe (bd-ofk1n)

Extends the two ancestor-symlink tests landed in 0a89a96a with three
adversarial shapes that 0a89a96a's fixtures don't exercise directly.
Each test keeps the existing hermetic-tempdir + unix-gated pattern
so there is no cross-test state and the suite still compiles on
non-unix targets (each test carries the existing \`#[cfg(unix)]\`
umbrella wherever needed). All three land under bead
coding_agent_session_search-ofk1n.

Tests added under \`mod cleanup_target_safety_tests\`:

- \`cleanup_target_safety_rejects_candidate_that_is_itself_a_symlink\`
  The prior fixtures always put the symlink on an ANCESTOR of the
  candidate path. This one builds a publish-backup parent that is
  a real directory, then creates a symlink AT the candidate path
  pointing to an external target. Exercises the distinct
  \`symlink_metadata(path).is_symlink()\` arm at the top of
  \`cleanup_path_has_symlink_below_root\` — a regression that only
  tested parents would accept this candidate and recursively
  \`rm -rf\` the symlink's target.

- \`cleanup_target_safety_rejects_hardlink_alias_pointing_at_db_path\`
  Stands up a real canonicalize-alias attack using
  \`std::fs::hard_link\` from a plausible \"retained DB backup\"
  location inside data_dir (so the string prefix check passes)
  back to the canonical \`agent_search.db\` file. Asserts the
  safety predicate still rejects the alias. Pins the
  canonicalize() post-check introduced in 0a89a96a — without it a
  hardlink alias would have slipped past the string-level
  \`starts_with(db_path)\` guard and caused the reclaimer to unlink
  the DB on disk.

- \`cleanup_target_safety_accepts_deep_nested_non_symlinked_candidate\`
  Builds a non-trivial retained-generation structure several
  directories deep under index_path — every ancestor a regular
  directory — and asserts the safety predicate ACCEPTS it. Pins
  the negative half of the contract: the symlink / canonicalize
  checks must not start rejecting legitimate deep paths as a
  side effect of a future refactor. Without this test, a regression
  that flipped the predicate closed for any non-trivial depth
  would go unnoticed until the reclaimer stopped cleaning up
  anything at all in real deployments.

Pure test additions. No production code paths touched.
